# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File (pg_hba.conf)
# ==========================================================================
# Production Database Authentication Rules
# ==========================================================================
#
# This file controls: which hosts are allowed to connect, how clients
# are authenticated, which PostgreSQL user names they can use, which
# databases they can access.
#
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD
#
# ==========================================================================
# Local Connections (Unix Domain Socket)
# ==========================================================================

# Allow the postgres superuser to connect locally via peer authentication
# (OS username must match PostgreSQL username)
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD
local:
  - description: "Superuser local access via peer authentication"
    type: local
    database: all
    user: postgres
    address: ""
    method: peer

  - description: "Admin role local access with scram-sha-256 password"
    type: local
    database: all
    user: admin
    address: ""
    method: scram-sha-256

  - description: "Application role local access with scram-sha-256 password"
    type: local
    database: appdb
    user: app
    address: ""
    method: scram-sha-256

  - description: "Read-only role local access with scram-sha-256 password"
    type: local
    database: appdb
    user: readonly
    address: ""
    method: scram-sha-256

  - description: "Deny all other local connections by default"
    type: local
    database: all
    user: all
    address: ""
    method: reject

# ==========================================================================
# Host-Based Connections (TCP/IP) - IPv4
# ==========================================================================
host_ipv4:
  # --- Admin Role Rules ---
  - description: "Admin access from management network"
    type: hostssl
    database: all
    user: admin
    address: 10.0.1.0/24
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-full

  - description: "Admin access from jump/bastion host"
    type: hostssl
    database: all
    user: admin
    address: 10.0.0.10/32
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-full

  # --- Application Role Rules ---
  - description: "App server tier 1 access to application database"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: app
    address: 10.0.10.0/24
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-ca

  - description: "App server tier 2 access to application database"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: app
    address: 10.0.11.0/24
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-ca

  - description: "App server tier 3 (Kubernetes pod network) access"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: app
    address: 10.0.20.0/22
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-ca

  # --- Read-Only Role Rules ---
  - description: "Read-only access from reporting/analytics servers"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: readonly
    address: 10.0.30.0/24
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-ca

  - description: "Read-only access from monitoring servers"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: readonly
    address: 10.0.31.0/24
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-ca

  - description: "Read-only access from BI tools network"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: readonly
    address: 10.0.32.0/24
    method: scram-sha-256

  # --- Replication Rules ---
  - description: "Replication user from standby server 1"
    type: hostssl
    database: replication
    user: replicator
    address: 10.0.2.11/32
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-full

  - description: "Replication user from standby server 2"
    type: hostssl
    database: replication
    user: replicator
    address: 10.0.2.12/32
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-full

  - description: "Replication user from standby server 3"
    type: hostssl
    database: replication
    user: replicator
    address: 10.0.2.13/32
    method: scram-sha-256
    options:
      clientcert: verify-full

  # --- Catch-All Deny Rule for IPv4 ---
  - description: "Reject all other IPv4 connections"
    type: host
    database: all
    user: all
    address: 0.0.0.0/0
    method: reject

# ==========================================================================
# Host-Based Connections (TCP/IP) - IPv6
# ==========================================================================
host_ipv6:
  - description: "Allow localhost IPv6 connections for health checks"
    type: hostssl
    database: appdb
    user: monitoring
    address: "::1/128"
    method: scram-sha-256

  - description: "Reject all other IPv6 connections"
    type: host
    database: all
    user: all
    address: "::0/0"
    method: reject

# ==========================================================================
# Configuration Metadata
# ==========================================================================
metadata:
  version: "1.0.0"
  environment: production
  last_updated: "2024-01-15"
  managed_by: "infrastructure-team"
  postgresql_version: "16"
  notes:
    - "All remote connections require SSL (hostssl)"
    - "Admin connections require full client certificate verification"
    - "Application connections require CA-verified client certificates"
    - "scram-sha-256 is used exclusively - md5 is not permitted"
    - "Default deny policy - all unmatched connections are rejected"
    - "Replication is limited to specific standby server IPs"

# ==========================================================================
# Network Definitions Reference
# ==========================================================================
network_reference:
  management_network: "10.0.1.0/24"
  bastion_host: "10.0.0.10/32"
  app_tier_1: "10.0.10.0/24"
  app_tier_2: "10.0.11.0/24"
  kubernetes_pods: "10.0.20.0/22"
  standby_servers: "10.0.2.0/24"
  reporting_servers: "10.0.30.0/24"
  monitoring_servers: "10.0.31.0/24"
  bi_tools_network: "10.0.32.0/24"

# ==========================================================================
# SSL Configuration Reference (configured in postgresql.conf)
# ==========================================================================
ssl_settings:
  ssl: "on"
  ssl_cert_file: "/etc/postgresql/ssl/server.crt"
  ssl_key_file: "/etc/postgresql/ssl/server.key"
  ssl_ca_file: "/etc/postgresql/ssl/ca.crt"
  ssl_crl_file: "/etc/postgresql/ssl/root.crl"
  ssl_min_protocol_version: "TLSv1.3"
  ssl_ciphers: "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"

# ==========================================================================
# Role Permissions Summary
# ==========================================================================
roles:
  admin:
    description: "Full administrative access to all databases"
    databases: all
    source_networks:
      - management_network
      - bastion_host
    auth_method: scram-sha-256
    ssl_required: true
    client_cert: verify-full
    max_connections: 5

  app:
    description: "Application read-write access to appdb only"
    databases:
      - appdb
    source_networks:
      - app_tier_1
      - app_tier_2
      - kubernetes_pods
    auth_method: scram-sha-256
    ssl_required: true
    client_cert: verify-ca
    max_connections: 200

  readonly:
    description: "Read-only access to appdb for reporting and analytics"
    databases:
      - appdb
    source_networks:
      - reporting_servers
      - monitoring_servers
      - bi_tools_network
    auth_method: scram-sha-256
    ssl_required: true
    client_cert: verify-ca
    max_connections: 50

  replicator:
    description: "Streaming replication to standby servers"
    databases:
      - replication
    source_networks:
      - standby_servers
    auth_method: scram-sha-256
    ssl_required: true
    client_cert: verify-full
    max_connections: 5

  monitoring:
    description: "Health check and monitoring access"
    databases:
      - appdb
    source_networks:
      - localhost_ipv6
    auth_method: scram-sha-256
    ssl_required: true
    client_cert: none
    max_connections: 3
